history.hypothesis: Remove the `<personality>` section's vague grading heuristic and redundant behavioral restatements, and trim the informational-only sentence from `<execution>`, to reduce noise the agent must parse.
history.critique: The current protocol scores 1.000, so nothing is broken. However, the `<personality>` section contains vague heuristics ("most first-pass builds are B- to C+") that could bias grading on non-benchmark tasks, and restates behaviors already defined in the execution protocol (referencing documents, tracking over time). The second sentence in `<execution>` is also informational noise — the agent reads the file in startup already and will discover its contents regardless. Removing these reduces cognitive load without changing any functional behavior.

revertTracking.hypothesis: Remove the `<personality>` section's vague grading heuristic and redundant behavioral restatements, and trim the informational-only sentence from `<execution>`, to reduce noise the agent must parse.
